dosages.adult: 650–1000 mg PO per protocol
dosages.pediatric: 15 mg/kg PO per protocol (max per local guideline)

dosages.adult: Per altitude illness / toxinology protocol
Topical/acetic acid irrigation per envenomation protocol
Per regional poison center protocol (AC loading/maintenance)
1 g/kg PO (usual adult dose 50–100 g) per poisoning protocol

name: Adenosine
indications: Regular monomorphic wide-complex tachycardia when indicated
contraindications: Second- or third-degree AV block (without pacemaker)

dosages.adult: 6 mg IV rapid push with NS flush (e.g. 10–20 mL); then 12 mg with flush if needed per tachycardia protocol
dosages.pediatric: 0.1 mg/kg IV rapid push with NS flush (first dose max 6 mg); subsequent 0.2 mg/kg with flush per protocol (max per guideline)

dosages.adult: 2.5–5 mg nebulized

name: Amiodarone
indications: Atrial fibrillation rate control when indicated

dosages.adult: Stable: 150 mg IV over 10 min; cardiac arrest: 300 mg IV/IO (may consider 150 mg repeat); infusion per post-ROSC care
dosages.pediatric: 5 mg/kg IV/IO (max 300 mg) for VF/pulseless VT per arrest protocol
dosages.maxDose: 300 mg bolus (arrest); 150 mg for stable wide-complex

name: Amyl Nitrite
contraindications: Smoke inhalation with suspected CO poisoning

dosages.adult: Per cyanide antidote kit / protocol
162–325 mg chewable PO (non–enteric-coated preferred)
dosages.pediatric: Not routinely for ACS in pediatrics—per protocol

name: Atropine
contraindications: None for ACLS tox scenarios (relative glaucoma, obstruction)

dosages.adult: Bradycardia: 1 mg IV q3–5min (max 3 mg) per bradycardia protocol
dosages.pediatric: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (single dose min 0.1 mg, max child dose 0.5 mg) q5min per protocol

dosages.adult: 1 g slow IV (10% solution) for hyperkalemia/CCB OD per protocol; pediatrics 20 mg/kg (0.2 mL/kg)
dosages.pediatric: 20 mg/kg IV (0.2 mL/kg of 10%) slow per protocol

dosages.adult: 1 g IV slow over 2 min; pediatrics 100 mg/kg (1 mL/kg of 10%)
dosages.pediatric: 100 mg/kg IV (1 mL/kg 10%) slow; may repeat per protocol

dosages.adult: Per allergic reaction protocol (adjunct to diphenhydramine)
0.1–0.6 mg/kg per croup/asthma protocol (agency-specific)
dosages.pediatric: 0.15–0.6 mg/kg IM/IV for croup per guideline

dosages.adult: D10 infusion or incremental boluses until mental status improves per hypoglycemia protocol
dosages.pediatric: D10 preferred all ages—incremental boluses per protocol (e.g. 5 mL/kg)

dosages.adult: 25–50 mL slow IV (typical) per hypoglycemia protocol
dosages.pediatric: Avoid D50 in pediatrics—use D10 per guideline

dosages.adult: 5–10 mg IV per seizure/behavioral protocol
dosages.pediatric: 0.2–0.3 mg/kg IV (max per protocol) for seizures

name: Diltiazem
indications: Stable narrow-complex SVT / rapid AF with RVR when appropriate

dosages.adult: 0.25 mg/kg IV over 2 minutes (repeat 0.35 mg/kg in 15 min per protocol)
dosages.pediatric: Not first-line in pediatrics—per medical direction
dosages.maxDose: typically adult bolus caps per agency

dosages.adult: 25–50 mg IV/IM/PO per anaphylaxis protocol
2–20 mcg/kg/min IV infusion titrate to MAP per shock protocol
Low-dose IM/IV per behavioral emergency protocol (monitor ECG)
Anaphylaxis: 0.3 mg IM lateral thigh; Neb: 5 mL of 1 mg/mL nebulized
dosages.pediatric: Anaphylaxis: 0.15 mg IM (<25 kg); Neb: per protocol

name: Epinephrine (0.1 mg/mL)
indications: Severe anaphylaxis IV drip after IM failures

dosages.adult: Arrest (adult ACLS): 1 mg IV/IO as soon as IV/IO access is established, then q3–5min; push-dose: 1 mL of 0.1 mg/mL in 9 mL NS then 0.01 mg/kg boluses per perfusion protocol
dosages.pediatric: Arrest: 0.01 mg/kg IV/IO (0.1 mL/kg of 0.1 mg/mL) q3–5min once access available; max per guideline

dosages.adult: 20 mg IV per anaphylaxis adjunct protocol
25–100 mcg slow IV titrated per pain protocol
dosages.pediatric: 1–2 mcg/kg slow IV per pain protocol (max per agency)

dosages.adult: Hypoglycemia: 1 unit IM; BB/CCB OD: 5 mg IV bolus then repeat per guideline
dosages.pediatric: Hypoglycemia 0.5–1 mg IM; BB/CCB: 0.05 mg/kg IV boluses per tox protocol

dosages.adult: 2–5 mg IM/IV per behavioral protocol (monitor QT)

name: Heliox (helium-oxygen)
indications: Severe bronchospasm refractory to standard care

dosages.adult: Heliox via tight-fitting mask / neb per respiratory distress protocol
5–10 mg IV slow per OB emergency protocol
100 mg IV bolus per adrenal crisis protocol
0.5–2 mg slow IV titrate per pain protocol
5 g IV over 15 min (kit dosing) per cyanide protocol
0.5 mg neb mixed with albuterol
Not routine; per nausea protocol
1–2 mg/kg IV or 4–5 mg/kg IM for sedation per protocol
15–30 mg IV/IM (max daily per policy)
10–20 mg IV slow; double dose q10min per severe hypertension protocol
Arrest: 1–1.5 mg/kg IV; repeat 0.5–0.75 mg/kg
0.1 mg/kg IV (max 4 mg) per seizure protocol
Torsades: 2 g IV over 1–2 min; OB: 4 g load per eclampsia protocol; bronchospasm: 2 g IV over 20 min
dosages.pediatric: Torsades 25–50 mg/kg; asthma 40 mg/kg max 2 g over 15–30 min per protocol

name: Methylprednisolone
contraindications: High-dose contraindicated TBI policy in some systems

dosages.adult: 40–125 mg IV per asthma protocol
10 mg IV slow
2.5–5 mg IV slow q5min (max 15 mg) per rate-control protocol
Seizure: 0.2 mg/kg IM (max 10 mg) or 0.1 mg/kg IV (max 4 mg) per guideline
dosages.pediatric: 0.2 mg/kg IM max 10; IV 0.1 mg/kg max 4 mg

dosages.adult: 2–4 mg slow IV titrate per pain/ACS protocol
IN/IM/IV 0.4–2 mg titrate to breathing (device-specific: 4 mg IN spray common)
30 mg XR PO per altitude protocol (immediate-release not for hypertensive emergency)
0.4 mg SL q3–5min if SBP >100; infusion per pulmonary edema protocol
50% N2O with O2 patient-controlled per pain protocol
0.02–0.4 mcg/kg/min IV titrate to MAP per shock protocol
5–10 mg IM per behavioral protocol (observe post-dose)
4–8 mg IV/IM/ODT
Topical intranasal per epistaxis protocol
130 mg PO daily during radiation emergency per public health dosing
dosages.pediatric: Age-based public health dosing (65 mg tablets crush)

dosages.adult: 1–2 g IV per tox protocol (autoinjector kit dosing)
Liquid equivalent per prednisone dose
40–60 mg PO per respiratory protocol
20–50 mg/min until arrhythmia suppressed or max 17 mg/kg—follow local infusion policy
dosages.pediatric: 15 mg/kg load over 30–60 min per protocol

dosages.adult: 5–10 mg IV/IM

name: Sildenafil
indications: HAPE adjunct to descent when prescribed

dosages.adult: Per altitude medicine protocol only
1 mEq/kg IV bolus for TCA OD or hyperkalemia per arrest/tox protocol
300 mg IV over 5+ minutes per Nithiodote kit (follow protocol)
12.5 g IV per kit following sodium nitrite

name: Sorbitol
indications: Historically with charcoal—not recommended co-administration per NASEMSO note

dosages.adult: Not routine—per poisoning specialist only
Per altitude medicine / MD order
10–20 mg IM per behavioral protocol

procedureData.parameters: Obtain within 10 minutes of contact for ACS suspicion
procedureData.successCriteria: Diagnostic-quality tracing transmitted or hand-delivered

procedureData.parameters: Follow device audio prompts; shock promptly when indicated; minimize pre- and peri-shock pause in compressions; resume CPR immediately
procedureData.successCriteria: VF/VT terminated or device advises no shock; CPR resumed immediately

procedureData.parameters: Adults (ACLS): ~**10 breaths/min** (1 breath q **6 s**) with **advanced airway** during CPR; **30:2** without. **Pediatrics (PALS):** with advanced airway use **continuous compressions** and **~1 breath every 2–3 s**; **30:2** or **15:2** without advanced airway per agency BLS. **Avoid excessive ventilation.**
procedureData.successCriteria: Visible chest rise; SpO2 improves or CO2 trace when monitored

procedureData.parameters: IPAP/EPAP per protocol; continuous SpO2 monitoring
procedureData.successCriteria: Reduced work of breathing; improved SpO2 and mentation

procedureData.parameters: Use EtCO2 numeric and waveform for tube confirmation and ROSC detection
procedureData.successCriteria: Appropriate waveform for clinical context; abrupt change investigated

procedureData.parameters: Lead II / 12-lead per presentation
procedureData.successCriteria: Rhythm classified; therapy matched to guideline

name: Occlusive chest seal
indications: Suspected tension pneumothorax with vented seal protocol
contraindications: None when indicated for penetrating trauma

procedureData.parameters: Three-sided or vented per protocol
procedureData.successCriteria: Seal adheres; ventilatory dynamics monitored

name: Continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP)
indications: Severe respiratory distress with spontaneous breathing

procedureData.parameters: Start 5–10 cm H2O typical; titrate per SpO2 and distress
procedureData.successCriteria: Improved SpO2 and reduced RR/work of breathing

procedureData.parameters: Adult: biphasic per manufacturer (often initial 120–200 J; if unknown use maximum available); monophasic 360 J; repeat equivalent or higher as appropriate; pediatric manual energy **2 J/kg** initial then **4 J/kg** subsequent (pediatric attenuator or joules/kg per device); minimize compression pause surrounding shock
procedureData.successCriteria: Rhythm no longer VF after shock; ROSC or organized rhythm

procedureData.parameters: Confirm machine calibration; repeat if discordant with clinical picture
procedureData.successCriteria: Accurate glucose documented and treated per guideline

name: Abdominal pain assessment and supportive care
contraindications: None for assessment—avoid blind palpation if AAA suspected

procedureData.parameters: Trend vitals; NPO; surgical abdomen considerations
procedureData.successCriteria: Pain trended; timely transport to appropriate facility

name: Chest pain / ACS / STEMI care bundle

procedureData.parameters: Aspirin 162–325 mg chewable; SL NTG if SBP >100; STEMI alert
procedureData.successCriteria: Aspirin documented; ECG <10 min; cath lab activation if indicated

name: Agitated / violent patient—de-escalation and sedation pathway

procedureData.parameters: Verbal de-escalation first; avoid prone restraint
procedureData.successCriteria: Patient calmer; crew and patient safe

procedureData.parameters: Head position; suction; **continuous waveform capnography when an advanced airway is placed**; escalate per difficulty
procedureData.successCriteria: Adequate oxygenation and ventilation

name: Altitude illness (AMS/HACE/HAPE) care
indications: High-altitude headache with ataxia or AMS

procedureData.parameters: Descent is definitive; oxygen; avoid sedatives depressing respiration
procedureData.successCriteria: Improved SpO2 and mental status during evacuation

name: Anaphylaxis and allergic reaction management
contraindications: None for epinephrine in life-threatening anaphylaxis

procedureData.parameters: Epinephrine IM early; IV fluids 20 mL/kg for hypoperfusion
procedureData.successCriteria: Blood pressure and airway improved after first-line therapy

procedureData.parameters: High-dose glucagon bolus then infusion; consider lipid emulsion per tox
procedureData.successCriteria: Perfusion improves with vasoactive support

name: Blast injury assessment and early care

procedureData.parameters: Primary blast: barotrauma; secondary: fragmentation; tertiary: blunt; quaternary: tox/burn
procedureData.successCriteria: Rapid triage; life threats addressed

name: Symptomatic bradycardia management
contraindications: None when unstable and pacing indicated

procedureData.parameters: Adults (ACLS): atropine 1 mg IV; repeat every 3–5 min to maximum 3 mg total; transcutaneous pacing if unstable or drugs inadequate; epinephrine or dopamine infusion if pacing delayed or ineffective. **PALS:** oxygen/ventilation first for symptomatic bradycardia; atropine **0.02 mg/kg IV/IO** (min 0.1 mg, max 0.5 mg) **may repeat once** in ≈5 min; TCP if unstable with poor perfusion; infusion if pacing delayed. Treat underlying causes.
procedureData.successCriteria: HR and perfusion improved

name: Pediatric bronchiolitis / viral wheeze support
contraindications: Routine bronchodilator may be ineffective—trial per protocol

procedureData.parameters: Hydration; nasal suction; escalate to advanced care if apnea or SpO2 fails
procedureData.successCriteria: Reduced retractions and improved SpO2

name: Brief resolved unexplained event (BRUE) evaluation
indications: Infant transient cyanosis or apnea resolved
contraindications: High-risk BRUE criteria require ED evaluation

procedureData.parameters: Risk stratify per newest guideline; observe during transport
procedureData.successCriteria: Stable vitals en route; appropriate disposition messaging

procedureData.parameters: Stop burning process; estimate TBSA; fluid bolus per pediatric policy
procedureData.successCriteria: Pain controlled; warm patient after focal cooling

procedureData.parameters: **Adults (ACLS):** High-quality CPR: depth ≥2 in (≈5 cm), rate 100–120/min, full recoil, minimize pauses, chest compression fraction >80% when measured, no excessive ventilation; 30:2 without advanced airway; with advanced airway continuous compressions with 1 breath every 6 s (~10/min); **waveform capnography** with advanced airway. Shock VF/pVT promptly; **biphasic defibrillation per manufacturer** (often **120–200 J** initially; **if unknown use maximum available**); **monophasic 360 J**; **epinephrine 1 mg IV/IO as soon as IV/IO access is established**, then **q3–5 min**; **amiodarone 300 mg IV/IO** then **150 mg IV/IO** (or **lidocaine 1–1.5 mg/kg** then **0.5–0.75 mg/kg**) for refractory VF/pVT. Do not defibrillate asystole/PEA. **PALS:** epinephrine **0.01 mg/kg IV/IO q3–5 min**; VF/pVT **2 J/kg** then **4 J/kg**; compress ≥1/3 AP depth at 100–120/min; **15:2** (two rescuers) or **30:2** (single) without advanced airway; with advanced airway **continuous compressions + ~1 breath every 2–3 s** (~20–30/min)—**avoid hyperventilation**; refractory VF/pVT **amiodarone 5 mg/kg** (max 300 mg).
procedureData.successCriteria: ROSC or organized termination per guideline

name: Calcium channel blocker overdose management
contraindications: None for calcium in suspected CCB overdose with shock

procedureData.parameters: Calcium chloride or gluconate boluses; pacing; lipids if high-dose verapamil/dilt
procedureData.successCriteria: BP and HR trend improved

procedureData.parameters: Dexamethasone per protocol; epinephrine neb for moderate–severe
procedureData.successCriteria: Stridor improves; child calm and SpO2 adequate

procedureData.parameters: Aggressive isotonic fluid prior to release when safe
procedureData.successCriteria: Vitals stable through extrication and transport

name: Cyanide exposure antidote pathway
indications: Smoke inhalation with suspicion of cyanide
contraindications: Amyl nitrite / sodium nitrite relatively contraindicated if isolated CO—use hydroxocobalamin per tox

procedureData.parameters: IV access; hydroxocobalamin 5 g adult; draw labs before if feasible
procedureData.successCriteria: Hemodynamics and mentation trend better with antidote

name: Dive / scuba injury and decompression illness
contraindications: Do not return to depth without hyperbaric consultation

procedureData.parameters: 100% O2; IV fluid; transport to hyperbaric-capable facility
procedureData.successCriteria: Symptoms stabilize or improve with O2 en route

procedureData.parameters: Spinal precautions if mechanism; standard BLS/CPR when pulseless—prioritize oxygenation/ventilation after submersion; oxygen; targeted temperature if ROSC
procedureData.successCriteria: Spontaneous breathing or ventilatory support adequate

name: Eclampsia / severe preeclampsia seizure care
contraindications: Repeat seizures—load magnesium per OB protocol

procedureData.parameters: Left lateral positioning; magnesium 4–6 g load per guideline; benzos if recurrent seizure
procedureData.successCriteria: Seizure stopped; BP trend controlled toward safe range

procedureData.parameters: ECG even if asymptomatic; prolonged monitoring per exposure voltage
procedureData.successCriteria: Stable rhythm; burns dressed; secondary survey complete

name: End-of-life / hospice comfort care transport
contraindications: Resuscitation when POLST indicates comfort measures only

procedureData.parameters: Align interventions with goals-of-care documentation
procedureData.successCriteria: Patient comfort honored; family expectations met

name: Bites / envenomation (including field irrigation)
contraindications: Tourniquet or ice for pit viper bite contraindicated per modern tox care

equipmentNeeded: Pressure immobilization only when protocol indicates species
procedureData.parameters: Dead reptile photo; mark swelling; venom extraction kits discouraged
procedureData.successCriteria: Systemic symptoms treated; rapid transport to antivenom center when indicated

name: General trauma—primary survey, hemorrhage, transport
contraindications: Scene delays—load and go when indicated

procedureData.parameters: ABCDE; 10 min scene goal when unstable
procedureData.successCriteria: Life threats addressed; appropriate trauma center

name: Traumatic brain injury—ICP considerations
contraindications: Hypoxia and hypotension worsen secondary injury

procedureData.parameters: Elevate head 30° when spine cleared; avoid hypotension
procedureData.successCriteria: GCS stable or improved; SpO2 >94%

name: Hyperthermia / heat stroke cooling
contraindications: Ice packs only without active cooling in heat stroke

procedureData.parameters: Rapid cooling; IV fluids when indicated
procedureData.successCriteria: Mental status improves with cooling

name: High threat / warm zone casualty care
contraindications: Care in cold zone only until secure

procedureData.parameters: Self-aid/buddy-aid; limited interventions; rapid extraction
procedureData.successCriteria: Casualty moved to casualty collection point alive

procedureData.parameters: Fluid resuscitation per guideline; insulin in hospital
procedureData.successCriteria: Hemodynamics support until ED insulin

name: Hypoglycemia treatment pathway
contraindications: Confirm glucose before large dextrose if uncertain

procedureData.parameters: D10 preferred; recheck glucose after treatment
procedureData.successCriteria: GCS normalizes with normoglycemia

name: Hypothermia rewarming and resuscitation
contraindications: Rough movement in severe hypothermia—arrest rhythms

procedureData.parameters: Gentle handling; active rewarming for moderate/severe
procedureData.successCriteria: Core temperature rises; perfusion improves

name: Lightning strike resuscitation
indications: Lightning strike victim apneic or pulseless
contraindications: Assume simultaneous triage—reverse triage if mass strike

procedureData.parameters: Aggressive BLS/ALS; prolonged resuscitation may succeed in lightning arrest
procedureData.successCriteria: ROSC or organized termination after extended effort if indicated

name: Ventricular assist device patient support
indications: LVAD patient with pump thrombosis concern
contraindications: Chest compressions only if directed by device team protocol

procedureData.parameters: Palpate for pulse; treat perfusion not numeric BP alone
procedureData.successCriteria: Device flow restored or patient at VAD center

procedureData.parameters: START/JumpSTART per jurisdiction; designate transport officer
procedureData.successCriteria: Most survivors matched to correct transport capacity

procedureData.parameters: Ondansetron / metoclopramide per protocol
procedureData.successCriteria: Symptoms reduced; able to tolerate transport

name: Neonatal resuscitation basics
indications: Newborn apneic or bradycardic after delivery

procedureData.parameters: Dry/warm/stimulate; suction if airway obstructed; **effective ventilation first**; CPAP if breathing effort poor—if HR remains <60 after adequate ventilation, **3:1** compressions:breaths per NRP-style neonatal CPR
procedureData.successCriteria: HR >100 with effective ventilation

procedureData.parameters: Repeat atropine until secretions dry; 2-PAM early
procedureData.successCriteria: Ventilation easier; secretions reduced

procedureData.parameters: Airway first; small repeat naloxone doses to spontaneous vent
procedureData.successCriteria: Adequate respiratory effort without violent withdrawal if avoidable

procedureData.parameters: Titrate to effect; re-assess q5min
procedureData.successCriteria: Pain score decreases; vitals stable

name: Poisoning / overdose universal assessment
contraindications: Activated charcoal when contraindicated—see tox Pearl

procedureData.parameters: Protect airway; identify agent; contact poison center
procedureData.successCriteria: Stable airway; packaging for transport

name: Post–return of spontaneous circulation care
contraindications: Sustained hyperoxia—titrate to SpO2 90–98% (PaO2 ~60–105 mm Hg) when reliable

procedureData.parameters: Maintain high FiO2 until SpO2 (or PaO2) is reliably measured, then titrate FiO2 for SpO2 90–98% (PaO2 ~60–105 mm Hg); ventilate toward PCO2 35–45 mm Hg; MAP ≥65 mm Hg; 12-lead for STEMI/ischemia; if not following commands off sedation/NMB, deliberate temperature control goal 32–37.5 °C ASAP
procedureData.successCriteria: BP and oxygenation supported en route to definitive care

equipmentNeeded: KI tablets if public health Mass Dispensing
procedureData.parameters: Time-distance-shielding; remove outer garments; nasal swabs at hospital
procedureData.successCriteria: Patient decontaminated; providers stay below exposure limits

procedureData.parameters: Position upright; neb; escalate to CPAP/NIV
procedureData.successCriteria: RR decreases; SpO2 improves

name: Inhalational irritant / chemical airway injury
contraindications: Enter confined space without HAZMAT clearance

procedureData.parameters: Humidified O2; bronchospasm treat like asthma; observe delayed pulmonary edema
procedureData.successCriteria: SpO2 maintained; wheeze improved

procedureData.parameters: Fresh air; skin decon; remove contact lenses
procedureData.successCriteria: Eye and respiratory symptoms improve

procedureData.parameters: Midazolam 0.2 mg/kg IM max 10 mg per guideline
procedureData.successCriteria: Seizure activity stops; airway protected

name: Shock—fluid and vasopressor strategy
contraindications: Fluid overload in flash pulmonary edema—vasopressors

procedureData.parameters: 20 mL/kg boluses pediatric; 1–2 L adult crystalloid per guideline
procedureData.successCriteria: MAP improved or signs of fluid responsiveness

procedureData.parameters: Rapid analgesia; avoid unnecessary hypoxia or cold
procedureData.successCriteria: Pain trend improved; hemodynamics stable

procedureData.parameters: Benzodiazepines first line; active cooling; watch CK/rhabdo
procedureData.successCriteria: Calm cooperative patient; core temp falling

procedureData.parameters: Document LKW; avoid unnecessary BP lowering unless protocol exception
procedureData.successCriteria: Notification to stroke center; transport without delay

name: Syncope / near-syncope evaluation
contraindications: STEMI must be ruled out when atypical

procedureData.parameters: Risk stratify; PE/AAA red flags
procedureData.successCriteria: High-risk patients transported; education for low-risk

name: Tachycardia with pulse—stable vs unstable
contraindications: Unstable = cardioversion; avoid adenosine for irregular wide

procedureData.parameters: Unstable tachycardia with serious signs → immediate synchronized cardioversion (or unsynchronized if VF/pVT); use **device-recommended energy or maximum biphasic output if unknown**. Stable regular narrow-complex → vagal maneuvers then **adenosine 6 mg rapid IV push with NS flush**, then **12 mg with flush** if needed; stable wide-complex VT → IV antiarrhythmic per ACLS (procainamide/amiodarone); irregular wide → avoid adenosine; polymorphic VT often unstable—defibrillate per ACLS. **PALS:** unstable SVT/V-Tach with pulse often **0.5–1 J/kg** synchronized initially; stable narrow regular → **weight-based adenosine** per PALS.
procedureData.successCriteria: Rate or rhythm controlled; perfusion restored

name: Conducted electrical weapon (CEW) injury assessment
contraindications: Prolonged restraint after CEW without assessment

procedureData.parameters: Assess for rhabdomyolysis, acidosis; evaluate traumatic fall injuries
procedureData.successCriteria: Rhythm stable; wounds managed; sedation if still combative

equipmentNeeded: HF calcium gluconate gel if protocol
procedureData.parameters: Prolonged irrigation; remove contaminated clothing; protect crew
procedureData.successCriteria: Pain decreases; pH normalized when measurable

procedureData.parameters: Patency first; oxygen through stoma
procedureData.successCriteria: Ventilation patent; SpO2 acceptable

name: Traumatic cardiac arrest protocol
indications: Tension pneumo suspected in trauma arrest
contraindications: Medical arrest algorithms alone without addressing trauma reversible causes

procedureData.parameters: CPR with high-quality breathing/circulation support while addressing reversible causes; bilateral decompress if indicated; hypovolemia resuscitation
procedureData.successCriteria: ROSC or transport for definitive surgical rescue

procedureData.parameters: Pack deep tracks; maintain pressure until definitive care
procedureData.successCriteria: Bleeding controlled or significantly reduced

name: Orotracheal intubation
contraindications: Anatomy predicts impossible laryngoscopy without backup

procedureData.parameters: ETT depth ~21–23 cm at teeth adult; confirm bilateral breath sounds + EtCO2
procedureData.successCriteria: Continuous EtCO2 waveform; SpO2 acceptable

procedureData.parameters: Proximal tibia or humerus adult; distal femur/tibia pediatric—per device
procedureData.successCriteria: Needle upright; flush easily; meds delivered

procedureData.parameters: Large-bore preferred in shock
procedureData.successCriteria: Patent IV; no signs of extravasation

name: Manual defibrillator operation
indications: Unstable tachycardia when synchronized mode unavailable initially
contraindications: Oxygen-rich env—brief pause ventilation during shock

procedureData.parameters: Adult: manufacturer-recommended biphasic energy (if unknown, maximum available); monophasic 360 J; pediatric arrest commonly **2 J/kg** then **4 J/kg** manual dosing per PALS; minimize compression pause
procedureData.successCriteria: Effective shock delivery with minimal compression pause

name: Mechanical ventilator transport
indications: Interfacility transport of intubated ventilated patient
contraindications: None when ventilator available and trained

procedureData.parameters: Match rate/tidal volume/PEEP to sending facility orders
procedureData.successCriteria: Stable EtCO2 and SpO2 throughout transport

procedureData.parameters: Verify landmark; saline coupling if jet ventilation
procedureData.successCriteria: Oxygenation improved; plan for definitive airway

name: Needle decompression tension pneumothorax
indications: Suspected tension pneumothorax with hemodynamic compromise

procedureData.parameters: 2nd ICS MCL or 4th–5th ICS anterior axillary per protocol
procedureData.successCriteria: Improved perfusion/BSI after decompression; reassess frequently

procedureData.parameters: Support controlled delivery; warm-dry-stimulate newborn
procedureData.successCriteria: Vital newborn; controlled cord; mother stabilized for transport

name: Physical / chemical restraint protocol
indications: Imminent danger to self or others when de-escalation fails
contraindications: None when safety imperative—monitor airway and circulation continuously

procedureData.parameters: Least restrictive effective; never prone
procedureData.successCriteria: Patient and crew safe; continuous assessment documented

procedureData.parameters: Preoxygenate; induction + paralytic per protocol
procedureData.successCriteria: EtCO2 waveform with ventilation; SpO2 maintained

name: Spinal motion restriction
contraindications: Clear spine criteria met (per local guideline)

equipmentNeeded: Backboard or scoop optional per policy
procedureData.parameters: Manual inline stabilization until collar sized
procedureData.successCriteria: Pain/numbness reassessed; immobilization tolerable

procedureData.parameters: Size per anthropometry; second verification with capnography
procedureData.successCriteria: Easy BVM ventilation; audible leak controlled

procedureData.parameters: Identify cricothyroid membrane; confirm with EtCO2
procedureData.successCriteria: Ventilation possible; hemorrhage controlled

name: Synchronized cardioversion
indications: SBP <90 with altered mental status from arrhythmia

procedureData.parameters: Adult: **synchronized mode**; use device-recommended energy—or **maximum biphasic output if unknown**—then escalate if refractory; sedate when feasible; **PALS:** unstable tachyarrhythmia with pulse often **0.5–1 J/kg** initial synchronized cardioversion then escalate per PALS
procedureData.successCriteria: Restoration of perfusing rhythm or improved perfusion

procedureData.parameters: High and tight; note time; only loosen in hospital
procedureData.successCriteria: Bleeding controlled; distal pulse absent expected

name: Transcutaneous pacing
indications: Hemodynamically unstable bradycardia unresponsive to atropine

procedureData.parameters: Start ~80 mA typical adult; titrate from lower output in pediatrics per capture; increase until capture; mA above capture for margin
procedureData.successCriteria: Electrical and mechanical capture; improved perfusion
